CHAMBER OF COMMERCE RE-

GIVES AN ACCOUNTING OF
GREAT RECORD OF BOOST
ING,

pages.raw_text:
The, greatest gathering of Spo-
kane business interests which ever
attended the annual chamber OF
commerce banquet assembled in
Masonic temple last night. Spokane
was boosted and boosted some
more in characteristic addresses.
The retiring trustees, Dave Ham,
e. F. G.. Van Dissel, F. r. gilbert
SON H. l.. Blocker and L. Mclean
were reelected.


R. B. Paterson spoke on Past
and Future from Commercial
Point of View; Mayor Moore on
Government" N w. Durham,
setting Results from Our Pub
licity Work.; Judge Edward Whit
son, irrigation in Washington and
the National Irrigation Congress"


The following members made ex-
temporaneous three minute speech
es: w. w. futile, mayor of Fer
ME, B. c.; G. M. Fassett, A. A..
Kraft, S. r. Stern and E. J. Can
non.


Moving pictures of scenes in the
Inland Empire which are to be
shown at the A-YP exposition
were exhibited


President F.. f. Goodall opened
the banquet with an address in
which he dwelt on the city's
growth during the past year.
